summary: Engine emit executes the SDK program over a node subprocess, receives the JSON payload, and becomes the sole compiler of projections + the whole lock; the SDK stops writing TOML

edit.description: `Command::Emit` (main.rs:367) runs the SDK program as a node subprocess and reads the erased declarations + prose as JSON on the pipe (the seam: 'the SDK implements no semantics; types, constructors, and one JSON pipe to the engine'). No harness re-supply; the payload IS the source.

`emit` (drift.rs:193) compiles EVERYTHING generated from the payload: every projection and the whole lock â€” all five declaration families (kind/clause/requirement/assembly/satisfies, drift.rs:930-934), closing the SDK's missing-`satisfies` gap. Double-emit byte-check stays (law 5). `render_emit` unchanged in shape.

Stop writing files: retire `writeEmit`'s `writeFileSync` for lock (emit.ts:252) and projections (emit.ts:256); `emit` returns the payload as JSON on stdout â€” the SDK's whole output surface. The SDK pins its engine version (the seam).

Retire the TOML lock writer (`stampLock`, lock.ts:154, and the rollup/declaration TOML serializers) â€” the engine compiles the lock now. The payload carries the fifth family (`satisfies`) so the engine writes all five.
Retire the SDK's TOML emitter â€” the byte-parity second writer the seam Decision rejects ('two implementations of one byte-contract drift apart'). No SDK code emits TOML after this slice.

Retire the SDK's projection writer (`projectBytes`/`projectMember`, project.ts:152/195) â€” projections are engine-owned from the payload. Placement-line helpers (`placementLines`) fold into S6's lock-grounded placement, not here.

tests.asserts: emit executes the SDK program and byte-reproduces projections + a five-family lock from the payload; double-emit is stable

acceptance: `temper emit` runs the SDK program via node, compiles every projection and the whole lock (five declaration families) from the JSON payload, double-emit verified; no SDK code writes TOML or projections.
notes: Chain head. Large but atomic: SDK-side (payload producer) and engine-side (sole compiler) are mutually dependent â€” the sdk-test + emit round-trip gates only pass together, so they land in one tick. Retires the parallel-writer asymmetry (ledger 'two byte-parity TOML writers'). The schemars/ts-rs interchange retires in S3 with its sole caller. Sole-producer per 20-surface 'The lock and drift'.

summary: The gate/explain member corpus becomes lock governs-columns â†’ discovery walk â†’ extraction; no lock falls back to the built-in lock; kill surface_units and live-extract

per.section: The lock and drift â€” one vocabulary

edit.description: The `Check --harness` branch (main.rs:286) and `session_start_diagnostics` (main.rs:567) resolve members from the committed lock: `KindFactRow.governs_root/glob` (drift.rs:724, written by every producer, read by nobody today) drives `discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin` (import.rs:691/569) then extraction. No lock â‡’ the built-in lock (embedded default program). Retire `skill_rule_corpus` (main.rs:958) + `live_extract_inplace` (main.rs:1014); drop the `check::surface_units` reads (main.rs:654,982,990,1130).

Retire `surface_units` (the copy-tree scratch reader) now that the gate reads lock governs-loci + the discovery walk; keep `empty_assembly_incoherence` (the fail-loud net) re-based onto the lock-read corpus.

tests.asserts: check resolves members by walking each lock kind-fact's governs locus; a harness with no lock is gated by the built-in lock, never a silent zero-member skip

acceptance: `temper check` reads declarations only from the committed lock, walks each kind's governs locus for members, and extracts them; no `surface_units` scratch read remains; no-lock falls back to the embedded default program (fail-loud on incoherence).
notes: Tests keep `import::run` as the transitional lock producer THIS slice (its `write_rollup` writes a full lock) so fixture churn defers to S3. Shares main.rs/check.rs with the chain â€” serialized behind S1. governs columns already written (drift.rs:724), just unread.

summary: Port the behavior-test setup from import::run to golden-lock or SDK-emitted fixtures, and delete the machinery-under-test test files the demolition retires

per.section: The lock and drift â€” one vocabulary

edit.description: Re-base setup from `import::run` to a golden-lock / SDK-emitted fixture (the gate reads the lock, not a scratch import).
Same re-base off `import::run` to a committed-lock fixture.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`; the no-lock fail-loud path now exercises the built-in-lock fallback.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run`.
Same re-base off `import::run` (install's own front-door test rewrites in S6; here only the lock-fixture setup).
Re-base onto the golden-lock fixture rather than an import round-trip.

Drop the `pub mod interchange;` line (src/schema.rs:47) â€” the schemars/ts-rs byte-parity interchange retires here with its sole caller tests/contract_fixtures.rs (S1 made the engine the sole compiler, dissolving the two-writer byte contract; the module stayed live only because contract_fixtures.rs still called it).

tests.asserts: the ported behavior tests exercise the gate over committed-lock fixtures, no import::run scaffolding

acceptance: The ~11 behavior-test files set up via golden-lock/SDK-emitted fixtures instead of `import::run`; the machinery-under-test files (adapter_fidelity, contract_fixtures, manifest_kind_spelling) and the schemars/ts-rs interchange module are deleted; `cargo test` green.
notes: Isolates fixture churn so S4 can delete `import::run` with no live callers. temper_toml.rs's manifest-emit block + genre_leaf's manifest round-trip are edited in S5 where their codec dies, not here. Shares the test tree â€” serialized behind S2.

summary: Delete the copy-tree scratch import pipeline (import::run, the surface writers, write_rollup, emit_manifest, carry_representation, scratch_surface); the discovery walk survives as the sole extractor

per.section: Decision: one authored surface, one implementation

edit.description: Delete `run`/`run_with_builtins` (import.rs:143/154), the copy-tree writers `write_member_surface`/`existing_surface_member`/`copy_companion` (621/670/840), `write_rollup` (866 â€” its transitional-producer job ended in S3), and `emit_manifest` (426). KEEP `discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin` (691/569) â€” the discovery walk is the sole extractor. `init`/`lift` (254/337) survive to S5.

Retire `carry_representation` (frontmatter.rs:318) and the custom-header carry twin â€” the authored-layer carry-forward is dead once members come from the lock + governs walk, not a re-imported surface.
Delete `scratch_surface` (main.rs:1076) and its two call sites (main.rs:287,568) â€” the `Check --harness` and session-start branches read the lock (S2), no scratch tree.

tests.asserts: emit + check operate with no copy-tree scratch import; the discovery walk is the only member extractor

acceptance: `import::run`, the copy-tree surface writers, `write_rollup`, `emit_manifest`, `carry_representation`, and `scratch_surface` are gone; the discovery walk (`discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin`) survives; `cargo test` green.
notes: Clean slate (John 07-06): no compat shim. Shares import.rs/frontmatter.rs/main.rs with the chain â€” serialized behind S3, which removed the last `import::run` test callers. `init`/`lift` deletion waits for S5 (the codec they drive).

summary: Retire the [[member]] manifest codec both directions plus AuthorLayer's member readers, and remove the init/lift verbs (their only remaining callers) under clean slate

per.section: Decision: install is the front door; the lift is its yes-path; the postures stay equal

edit.description: Retire the `[[member]]` codec both directions: writers `write_members`/`member_to_table` and the genre/section sub-writers (compose.rs:1512-1656), and parsers `parse_member`/`parse_member_fields`/`parse_member_sections`/`parse_member_fenced`/`parse_member_genres`/`parse_member_published` (1738-1980). Retire `AuthorLayer::members()`/`inplace_members()` (their consumers die with init/lift). Keep the `[role.*]`/severity layer if still read elsewhere; S7 finishes it.
Remove the `init`/`lift` verbs (import.rs:254/337) â€” the codec's only remaining callers. Clean slate: a temporary on-ramp gap on trunk is acceptable until S6 ships the front door.
Drop the `Command::Init` variant + dispatch (main.rs:256) and its clap definition; `install` is the sole on-ramp (S6). No `init` alias survives (clean slate).

tests.asserts: genre leaf addressing survives without the [[member]] manifest round-trip block

acceptance: The `[[member]]` codec (both directions), `AuthorLayer`'s member readers, and the `init`/`lift` verbs are gone; `temper.toml` as a manifest dialect is unreadable; `cargo test` green (tests/temper_toml.rs deleted, genre_leaf's manifest block edited out).
notes: Clean slate (John 07-06): `init` may be removed before the front door ships â€” a trunk on-ramp gap is acceptable, green gates the only bar. Shares compose.rs/import.rs/main.rs â€” serialized behind S4. genre_leaf.rs manifest round-trip block trimmed here.

summary: Rebuild install as the one on-ramp: discovery report â†’ one question â†’ scaffold the SDK program + first emit on yes, reporter alone on no; guard/note grounded in the lock

per.section: install â€” the front door; the lift, once

edit.description: (a) Scaffolder: discovery walk â†’ generated TS member modules (`file()` pointers at the original paths, bytes untouched) + `harness.ts` skeleton â€” the lift's output is the SDK program and nothing else, no lock. (b) Verb: discovery report first, one question 'represent this project?' (`--yes` for agents/CI, `--no-represent`); no-path = consented SessionStart reporter alone (Node-free); yes-path = require Node+`.temper` (hard refusal with instructions when absent), ensure `@dtmd/temper` dep, scaffold, run first emit (the adoption moment), place per lock. (c) Guard/note lock-grounded: rewrite `GUARD_MESSAGE` (install.rs:101) and the managed-by note off the lock's emit-owned rows; place the modeline only when its schema artifact exists, the guard only when emit-owned paths exist ('the guard arrives with its constituency').
`Command::Install` dispatch carries the one-question flow (`--yes`/`--no-represent`); `Command::Guard`'s posture reads the lock, not a `temper.toml` AuthorLayer (main.rs:390).

tests.asserts: install reports findings, asks once; no-path wires only the session-start reporter; yes-path scaffolds the SDK program, runs first emit, places note/guard only for lock-declared emit-owned paths; an all-in-place member gets no guard claim

acceptance: `temper install` is the sole on-ramp: report â†’ one question â†’ reporter-alone on no, or scaffold+first-emit+lock-grounded placements on yes; guard/note bind only lock-declared emit-owned paths; a `file()`-own-path member is authored territory (no guard). Cascade's report (1) dissolves.
notes: Resolves `(carriage-aware-placements)` in code (the lock names a path a projection â€” 20-surface 'surface authority is a declared posture'). Depends on the built-in lock (S1) + lock-read gate (S2) + codec/init removal (S5). Shares install.rs/main.rs â€” serialized behind S5.

summary: Eradicate every remaining temper.toml reader â€” AuthorLayer load/authority/fold_local, the TEMPER_TOML const, explain's load_layer residue â€” the manifest filename is gone

per.section: The lock and drift â€” one vocabulary

edit.description: Retire `AuthorLayer::load`/`authority`/`fold_local` and the `TEMPER_TOML` const â€” no verb reads a `temper.toml`. Any `[role.*]`/severity layer S5 left standing retires here if nothing reads it.
Remove the `TEMPER_TOML` usages in `explain` (main.rs:439, `load_layer`) and any guard/check residue â€” posture and layers come from the lock.

tests.asserts: no verb reads or writes a temper.toml; a file by that name is inert (old temper, never current temper's input)

acceptance: `rg -c 'temper\.toml' src/ tests/` = 0; the manifest filename is retired entirely (20-surface: 'the name temper.toml retires with the manifest era entirely'). Cascade re-onboards via the new install; its manifest-era artifacts are old temper by definition.
notes: Chain terminal. Cascade's reports (2)-(4) â€” the accepted-debt DATUM under `(inplace-lock-producer)` â€” close with this slice: no manifest-mode coverage remains. Shares compose.rs/main.rs â€” serialized behind S6.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels â€” SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle â€” fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup â€” the per-platform engine-binary release workflow, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds â€” before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end; the SDK scope is resolved (`@dtmd/temper` published 0.0.2 on 07-06, subpath layout ratified PR #4), and channel 1's SDK foundation has shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published â€” the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.2 07-06); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
